- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

1	"""
2	reasoning_client — the ADR-0016 LLM execution-routing seam.
3	
4	A single abstraction that isolates LLM auth/transport from agent logic. Agents
5	declare *what* to reason about and at *what stakes tier*; this seam decides
6	*where* the call runs, how it authenticates, how it retries, times out, and how
7	it is traced. No agent imports an LLM client directly — that coupling lives here
8	and only here (ADR-0016 §"Implementation seam").
9	
10	Tiers (routing is by stakes, per ADR-0016 / agents/MODEL_TIER_ASSIGNMENTS.md):
11	
12	    Tier.T1  — Deterministic ML. No LLM. complete() on T1 is a routing bug and
13	               raises: T1 work is computation, never inference.
14	    Tier.T2  — Local LLM on the Mac mini, 24/7 (Ollama). Bulk / low-stakes
15	               reasoning: per-pair classification, triage, drafting, review.
16	    Tier.T3  — Frontier Claude Max 20x via claude-agent-sdk. The `claude` CLI's
17	               subscription OAuth (CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN) is the ONLY auth path
18	               — NO Anthropic API key. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is scrubbed from the SDK
19	               subprocess env so a stray key can never spill to the metered API
20	               (ADR-0016). Reserved for high-stakes judgments. Bursty / queued.
21	
22	Reliability (hardening pass 1):
23	  - Every call has a per-tier timeout (#145, #158) so a hung backend can never
24	    block a synchronous LangGraph node forever.
25	  - Transient failures are retried with bounded exponential backoff (#157).
26	  - Every call is wrapped in an OpenTelemetry span carrying run_id/agent_id/tier
27	    (OpenInference LLM conventions) so BOTH tiers are visible in Phoenix — T3 is
28	    not LangChain-instrumented, so without this it would be invisible (#150, #168).
29	    Spans are cheap no-ops when no tracer provider is registered (offline/tests).
30	  - complete_json uses Ollama's native JSON mode on T2 and a balanced-brace
31	    extractor, instead of a greedy regex that mangled multi-value replies (#159).

609	def complete_json(
610	    tier: Tier,
611	    prompt: str,
612	    *,
613	    system: str | None = None,
614	    temperature: float = 0.0,
615	    max_turns: int = 1,
616	    model: str | None = None,
617	    run_id: str | None = None,
618	    agent_id: str | None = None,
619	) -> Any:
620	    """
621	    Like complete(), but parse the model's reply as JSON.
622	
623	    On T2 this uses Ollama's native JSON mode (constrained decoding). The reply is
624	    then parsed, falling back to a balanced-brace extractor for models that wrap
625	    JSON in prose/fences. A parse failure is retried (re-generating the reply) up
626	    to MAX_RETRIES — the retry budget belongs here, not only in complete() (#230).
627	    Each retry bumps the decoding temperature (#275): with a fixed seed + temp 0,
628	    Ollama decoding is deterministic, so re-issuing the identical prompt would yield
629	    a byte-identical bad reply and the regenerate-on-parse-failure contract could
630	    never recover. A small bump yields a distinct cached client and a genuinely
631	    different generation. (T3 ignores temperature and is non-deterministic anyway.)
632	    Raises ReasoningError if no valid JSON can be recovered.
633	    """
634	    json_system = (system + "\n\n" if system else "") + (
635	        "Respond with ONLY a single valid JSON value and no other text, "
636	        "no markdown fences, no commentary."
637	    )
638	    # Fall back to the run_id stamped in OTel baggage, so the complete_json span
639	    # carries it even when the caller didn't pass it — mirrors complete() (pass-7).
640	    if run_id is None:
641	        try:
642	            from opentelemetry import baggage
643	            run_id = baggage.get_baggage("hcgrc.run_id") or None
644	        except Exception:
645	            run_id = None
646	    last_exc: ReasoningError | None = None
647	    # Span the parse-retry loop so bad-JSON regenerations are visible in Phoenix
648	    # rather than silent (#269).
649	    with _tracer.start_as_current_span("reasoning.complete_json") as span:
650	        span.set_attribute("hcgrc.tier", tier.value)
651	        if run_id:
652	            span.set_attribute("hcgrc.run_id", run_id)
653	            span.set_attribute("session.id", run_id)  # mirror complete() (pass-6)
654	        if agent_id:
655	            # Mirror complete()'s child span so the parent carries the same
656	            # correlation id, not just its children (pass-5).
657	            span.set_attribute("hcgrc.agent_id", agent_id)
658	        for attempt in range(MAX_RETRIES + 1):
659	            # Vary decoding across regenerations so a retry can actually differ from
660	            # the prior attempt (#275). A plain min(1.0, temp + bump) saturates at
661	            # 1.0 for high input temperatures, making retries identical again
662	            # (pass-5); when the upward bump would exceed 1.0, bump DOWNWARD instead
663	            # so every attempt gets a distinct, in-range temperature.
664	            if attempt == 0:
665	                attempt_temp = temperature
666	            else:
667	                bump = 0.2 * attempt
668	                attempt_temp = (temperature + bump if temperature + bump <= 1.0
669	                                else max(0.0, temperature - bump))
670	            try:
671	                raw = complete(
672	                    tier, prompt, system=json_system, temperature=attempt_temp,
673	                    max_turns=max_turns, model=model, run_id=run_id, agent_id=agent_id,
674	                    _json_mode=True,
675	                )
676	            except ReasoningError as exc:
677	                # A backend failure from complete() (rate limit / permanent / other,
678	                # already internally retried) is NOT a JSON parse failure — record it
679	                # on this span too (else only complete()'s child span has it) and
680	                # propagate (pass-5).
681	                span.record_exception(exc)
682	                span.set_status(trace.StatusCode.ERROR, str(exc))
683	                raise
684	            try:
685	                result = _extract_json(raw)
686	                span.set_attribute("json.parse_attempts", attempt + 1)
687	                return result
688	            except ReasoningError as exc:
689	                last_exc = exc  # bad/garbled JSON — regenerate and try again
690	                span.add_event("json_parse_failure",
691	                               {"attempt": attempt, "error": str(exc)[:300]})
692	        # Record the attempt count on the failure path too, mirroring the success
693	        # path (line above) and complete()'s symmetric llm.retries (pass-10).
694	        span.set_attribute("json.parse_attempts", MAX_RETRIES + 1)
695	        span.record_exception(last_exc)
696	        span.set_status(trace.StatusCode.ERROR, str(last_exc))
697	        raise last_exc
698	
699	
700	def _extract_json(text: str) -> Any:
701	    """Recover a JSON value from a model reply that may include fences/prose."""
702	    candidate = text.strip()
703	    # Strip a ```json ... ``` fence if present.
704	    if candidate.startswith("```"):
705	        inner = candidate[3:]
706	        if inner[:4].lower() == "json":
707	            inner = inner[4:]
708	        candidate = inner.rsplit("```", 1)[0].strip()
709	    try:
710	        return json.loads(candidate)
711	    except json.JSONDecodeError:
712	        pass
713	    span = _first_balanced_json(candidate)
714	    if span is not None:
715	        try:
716	            return json.loads(span)
717	        except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
718	            raise ReasoningError(f"Reply was not valid JSON: {text[:200]!r}") from exc
719	    raise ReasoningError(f"No JSON value found in reply: {text[:200]!r}")
720	
721	
722	def _first_balanced_json(text: str) -> str | None:
723	    """
724	    Return the first balanced {...} or [...] span, respecting string literals.
725	
726	    A greedy regex (`[\\{\\[].*[\\}\\]]`) grabs from the first brace to the LAST
727	    brace, which mangles replies containing multiple JSON values or braces in
728	    trailing prose. This walks the structure honestly (#159).
729	    """
730	    start = None
731	    for i, ch in enumerate(text):
732	        if ch in "{[":
733	            start = i
734	            break
735	    if start is None:
736	        return None
737	    open_ch = text[start]
738	    close_ch = "}" if open_ch == "{" else "]"
739	    depth = 0
740	    in_str = False
741	    escaped = False
742	    for i in range(start, len(text)):
743	        ch = text[i]
744	        if in_str:
745	            if escaped:
746	                escaped = False
747	            elif ch == "\\":
748	                escaped = True
749	            elif ch == '"':
750	                in_str = False
751	            continue
752	        if ch == '"':
753	            in_str = True
754	        elif ch == open_ch:
755	            depth += 1
756	        elif ch == close_ch:
757	            depth -= 1
758	            if depth == 0:
759	                return text[start:i + 1]
760	    return None
761	
762	
763	def is_available(tier: Tier) -> bool:
764	    """
765	    Cheap, side-effect-light liveness check for a tier's backend, used by nodes
766	    that must degrade gracefully rather than fail the run.
767	
768	    Does NOT spend an LLM turn (the old probe burned a real Tier-3 frontier turn
769	    on every call, #174, and could hang with no timeout, #173/#30). T1 is always
770	    available (local computation). Honors HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING.
771	    """
772	    if tier is Tier.T1:
773	        return True
774	    if os.environ.get("HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING", "").strip():
775	        return False
776	    if tier is Tier.T2:
777	        # ADR-0002: even the liveness probe must not hit a non-loopback endpoint —
778	        # guard T2_BASE_URL here too, mirroring _t2_complete (pass-8). Fail loud on
779	        # a misconfigured remote host rather than silently probing it off-box.
780	        from .infrastructure.config import require_local_uri
781	        require_local_uri(T2_BASE_URL, "Tier-2 Ollama (HCGRC_OLLAMA_BASE_URL)")
782	        # Hit Ollama's /api/tags — no generation, short timeout — and confirm the
783	        # configured model is actually pulled, not merely that the server is up (#210).
784	        import json as _json
785	        import urllib.request
786	        try:
787	            with urllib.request.urlopen(f"{T2_BASE_URL}/api/tags", timeout=3) as r:
788	                if r.status != 200:
789	                    return False
790	                names = [m.get("name", "") for m in _json.loads(r.read()).get("models", [])]
791	        except Exception:
792	            return False
793	        # Exact match only: a prefix match (e.g. configured llama3.1:8b vs a
794	        # pulled llama3.1:70b) would pass here but complete() would then fail as
795	        # "model not found" (#232). Ollama tags an untagged pull as ":latest".
796	        return any(n == T2_MODEL or n == f"{T2_MODEL}:latest" for n in names)
797	    if tier is Tier.T3:
798	        # The `claude` CLI must be present and an OAuth token / stored creds
799	        # available — no frontier turn spent.

1	"""
2	Platform configuration loader.
3	
4	Single source of truth for reading configs/platform.yaml. Infrastructure
5	modules use this so host/port/uri parameters are never hardcoded — they
6	track the version-controlled config.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import functools
12	from pathlib import Path
13	from typing import Any
14	from urllib.parse import urlparse
15	
16	import yaml
17	
18	
19	def repo_root() -> Path:
20	    """Return the repository root (three levels up from this file)."""
21	    return Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
22	
23	
24	class ConfigError(ValueError):
25	    """Raised when configs/platform.yaml is malformed or missing required keys."""
26	
27	
28	# ── ADR-0002 data-sovereignty: the canonical loopback guard ───────────────────
29	# SCF-derived content (embeddings, traces, experiment metadata) must NEVER leave
30	# the compute node. This is the single shared validator used by EVERY infra module
31	# that takes a host/URI from config — Phoenix, Qdrant, MLflow — so the boundary is
32	# enforced identically everywhere instead of one guard per module (pass-5).
33	LOOPBACK_HOSTS = {"localhost", "127.0.0.1", "::1"}
34	
35	
36	def require_loopback(host: str, what: str) -> str:
37	    """Return host if it is loopback, else raise ConfigError. 0.0.0.0 is rejected:
38	    it binds all interfaces, exposing data off-box — exactly what ADR-0002 forbids."""
39	    if host not in LOOPBACK_HOSTS:
40	        raise ConfigError(
41	            f"{what} host {host!r} is not loopback. SCF-derived content must stay on "
42	            f"the compute node (ADR-0002). Use one of {sorted(LOOPBACK_HOSTS)}."
43	        )
44	    return host
45	
46	
47	def require_local_uri(uri: str, what: str) -> str:
48	    """Validate a backing-store URI is local (ADR-0002). A bare path or sqlite:///
49	    file is local; any networked scheme (http/https/postgresql/mysql/mssql) must
50	    point at a loopback host. Rejects remote tracking servers that would exfiltrate."""
51	    if uri.startswith("sqlite:") or "://" not in uri:
52	        return uri  # local sqlite file or a bare relative directory store
53	    host = (urlparse(uri).hostname or "").strip()
54	    # No host component (e.g. file:///path) is local; a host must be loopback.
55	    if host and host not in LOOPBACK_HOSTS:
56	        raise ConfigError(
57	            f"{what} URI host {host!r} is not loopback ({uri!r}). Experiment/tracking "
58	            f"data must stay on the compute node (ADR-0002)."
59	        )
60	    return uri
61	
62	
63	# Required structure (dotted paths). Validated at load so a silent typo or a
64	# dropped section fails loudly at startup rather than as a confusing KeyError deep
65	# in an infrastructure module (#171).
66	_REQUIRED_PATHS = (
67	    "observability.phoenix.port",
68	    "observability.phoenix.project_name",
69	    "observability.mlflow.tracking_uri",
70	    "vector_store.host",       # dereferenced by qdrant_setup (#207)
71	    "vector_store.http_port",
72	    "checkpointing.backend",   # drives PostgresSaver selection (#271)
73	    "checkpointing.database",
74	)
75	
76	
77	def _validate_config(cfg: Any, source: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
78	    if not isinstance(cfg, dict):
79	        raise ConfigError(f"{source}: top-level YAML must be a mapping, got {type(cfg).__name__}.")
80	    for path in _REQUIRED_PATHS:
81	        node: Any = cfg
82	        for key in path.split("."):
83	            if not isinstance(node, dict) or key not in node:
84	                raise ConfigError(f"{source}: missing required config key '{path}'.")
85	            node = node[key]
86	        # A key present but null (YAML `key:` with no value) is as broken as a
87	        # missing one — it sails through the presence check then blows up downstream
88	        # as a TypeError. Reject it here (pass-7).
89	        if node is None:
90	            raise ConfigError(f"{source}: required config key '{path}' is null.")
91	
92	    # checkpointing.backend selects the checkpointer; an unrecognized value silently
93	    # falls through to the Postgres path. Validate the enum at load (pass-7).
94	    backend = cfg["checkpointing"]["backend"]
95	    if backend not in ("memory", "postgres"):
96	        raise ConfigError(
97	            f"{source}: checkpointing.backend must be 'memory' or 'postgres', got {backend!r}."
98	        )
99	
100	    # ADR-0002 fail-fast: enforce loopback/local on every host/URI value at LOAD
101	    # time, not just at point-of-use, so a misconfigured platform.yaml is rejected
102	    # at startup rather than silently exfiltrating to a remote host (pass-5). These
103	    # are value checks; the loop above only checked key presence.
104	    require_loopback(str(cfg["vector_store"]["host"]), f"{source}: vector_store")
105	    require_local_uri(
106	        str(cfg["observability"]["mlflow"]["tracking_uri"]), f"{source}: observability.mlflow"
107	    )
108	    phoenix = cfg["observability"].get("phoenix", {})
109	    if isinstance(phoenix, dict) and "host" in phoenix:
110	        require_loopback(str(phoenix["host"]), f"{source}: observability.phoenix")
111	    checkpointing = cfg.get("checkpointing", {})
112	    if isinstance(checkpointing, dict) and "host" in checkpointing:
113	        require_loopback(str(checkpointing["host"]), f"{source}: checkpointing")
114	    return cfg
115	
116	
117	@functools.lru_cache(maxsize=1)
118	def load_platform_config() -> dict[str, Any]:
119	    """
120	    Load, validate, and cache configs/platform.yaml.
121	
122	    Cached for the process lifetime — platform-scope config is stable within a
123	    run. Call load_platform_config.cache_clear() in tests that mutate the file.
124	    Raises ConfigError if required sections are missing.
125	    """
126	    config_path = repo_root() / "configs" / "platform.yaml"
127	    if not config_path.exists():
128	        raise FileNotFoundError(
129	            f"Platform config not found at {config_path}. "
130	            "Infrastructure modules require configs/platform.yaml."
131	        )
132	    with config_path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
133	        cfg = yaml.safe_load(fh)
134	    return _validate_config(cfg, str(config_path))

1	"""
2	PostgresSaver configuration for HC-GRC.
3	
4	LangGraph uses the checkpointer to persist graph state between turns,
5	enabling interrupt/resume across the operator approval gates.
6	
7	Per ADR-0015 (#79): run_id propagates to PostgresSaver as thread_id.
8	Cross-store query procedure in RUNBOOK.md §5.
9	
10	LOCAL-FIRST CONSTRAINT: PostgreSQL instance runs on-device.
11	No state leaves the machine. SaaS checkpointers are disqualified.
12	
13	Connection config:
14	  Database: hcgrc
15	  Host: localhost (default)
16	  Port: 5432 (default)
17	
18	For Phase 0 testing: use MemorySaver (no Postgres required).
19	For Phase 1+: use PostgresSaver with the connection string below.
20	"""
21	
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	import atexit
25	import os
26	import re
27	import threading
28	
29	from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
30	
31	# Arbitrary fixed key for the Postgres session advisory lock that serializes
32	# checkpoint schema migrations across concurrent initializers (get_checkpointer).
33	_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY = 0x4843_4752  # "HCGR"
34	
35	# Guards the process-local one-time-init bookkeeping below. The PG advisory lock
36	# serializes the DDL across PROCESSES, but the in-memory check-and-set guards
37	# (_atexit_registered, _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS) are not atomic on their own — two
38	# threads calling get_checkpointer() concurrently could both pass the check and
39	# double-register the atexit handler / double-run setup(). This lock makes those
40	# check-and-set sequences atomic within the process (pass-5).
41	_init_lock = threading.Lock()
42	
43	# Pools opened by get_checkpointer, closed once at process exit. A single atexit
44	# handler closes them all — registering one per call would accumulate handlers
45	# across repeated get_checkpointer() calls (#249).
46	_OPEN_POOLS: list = []
47	_atexit_registered = False
48	
49	# conn_strs whose schema bootstrap (advisory lock + setup() DDL + migration SELECT)
50	# has already run in this process. setup() is idempotent but not free — re-running
51	# it on every get_checkpointer() call takes a global advisory lock and replays the
52	# migration version query at each run start. Bootstrap once per process per schema;
53	# the advisory lock still guards the first-time cross-process migration race (#276).
54	_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS: set[str] = set()
55	
56	
57	# Strips `://user:password@` (URI userinfo) from any URI-like substring. Allows '/'
58	# inside the userinfo so a password containing '/' is still redacted; only '@' (the
59	# userinfo terminator) and whitespace bound the match (pass-10 + its review).
60	_CREDS_RE = re.compile(r"://[^@\s]+@")
61	
62	
63	def _redact_creds(text: str, conn_str: str = "") -> str:
64	    """Remove credentials a backend exception (e.g. psycopg OperationalError) may
65	    embed in its message text. Two layers: an exact replacement of the known
66	    conn_str (precise, handles any password characters incl. spaces), then a generic
67	    URI-userinfo strip as a backstop for any other URI in the text."""
68	    if conn_str:
69	        text = text.replace(conn_str, _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str))
70	    return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", text)
71	
72	
73	def _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str: str) -> str:
74	    """Return scheme://host:port/db with any user:password userinfo stripped, so a
75	    connection string carrying credentials is never surfaced in an error message —
76	    the conn_str is documented "never logged" (pass-10). IPv6 hosts are re-bracketed."""
77	    from urllib.parse import urlsplit
78	    try:
79	        u = urlsplit(conn_str)
80	        host = u.hostname or ""
81	        if ":" in host:  # IPv6 literal — urlsplit drops the brackets; restore them
82	            host = f"[{host}]"
83	        netloc = f"{host}:{u.port}" if u.port else host
84	        return f"{u.scheme}://{netloc}{u.path}" if u.scheme else "<postgres>"
85	    except Exception:
86	        return "<postgres>"
87	
88	
89	def _safe_close(pool) -> None:
90	    """Close one pool, swallowing errors so a single failing close() does not abort
91	    cleanup of the remaining pools at interpreter exit (pass-6). close() ultimately
92	    calls a C finish() that can raise."""
93	    try:
94	        pool.close()
95	    except Exception:
96	        pass
97	
98	
99	def _register_pool_cleanup(pool) -> None:
100	    global _atexit_registered
101	    with _init_lock:
102	        _OPEN_POOLS.append(pool)
103	        if not _atexit_registered:
104	            atexit.register(lambda: [_safe_close(p) for p in _OPEN_POOLS])
105	            _atexit_registered = True
106	
107	# ── Connection string ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
108	
109	def get_postgres_connection_string() -> str:
110	    """
111	    Return the PostgreSQL connection string.
112	
113	    Precedence: HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL env var → the checkpointing.{host,port,database}
114	    section of platform.yaml → localhost default. This makes the platform.yaml
115	    checkpointing config live instead of dead (#225). Never logged, never committed.
116	
117	    ADR-0002: the resolved URI MUST point at a loopback host — graph state holds the
118	    full corpus analysis / hypotheses / gate decisions and must not leave the box.
119	    The env-var path bypasses config-load validation, so it is guarded here too
120	    (point-of-use defense-in-depth, mirroring Qdrant/MLflow) (pass-6).
121	    """
122	    from .infrastructure.config import require_local_uri
123	
124	    env_url = os.environ.get("HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL")
125	    if env_url:
126	        return require_local_uri(env_url, "checkpointing (HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL)")
127	    try:
128	        from .infrastructure.config import load_platform_config
129	        cp = load_platform_config().get("checkpointing", {}) or {}
130	        host = cp.get("host", "localhost")
131	        port = cp.get("port", 5432)
132	        database = cp.get("database", "hcgrc")
133	        url = f"postgresql://{host}:{port}/{database}"
134	    except Exception:
135	        return "postgresql://localhost:5432/hcgrc"
136	    # Validate OUTSIDE the try so a non-loopback host raises rather than being
137	    # masked by the localhost fallback.
138	    return require_local_uri(url, "checkpointing")
139	
140	
141	def configured_checkpointer():
142	    """
143	    Return the checkpointer the platform config asks for: MemorySaver when
144	    checkpointing.backend == 'memory', else the pooled PostgresSaver (#226).
145	    Runners use this as the default instead of hardcoding in-memory.
146	    """
147	    backend = "postgres"
148	    try:
149	        from .infrastructure.config import load_platform_config
150	        backend = (load_platform_config().get("checkpointing", {}) or {}).get("backend", "postgres")
151	    except Exception:
152	        backend = "postgres"
153	    return get_checkpointer(use_memory=(backend == "memory"))
154	
155	
156	# ── Checkpointer factory ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
157	
158	def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False):
159	    """
160	    Return a configured checkpointer.
161	
162	    Args:
163	        use_memory: If True, return MemorySaver (Phase 0 testing, no Postgres needed).
164	                    If False, return PostgresSaver (Phase 1+ production).
165	
166	    Returns:
167	        MemorySaver | PostgresSaver
168	    """
169	    if use_memory:
170	        return MemorySaver()
171	
172	    try:
173	        from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
174	        from psycopg.rows import dict_row
175	        from psycopg_pool import ConnectionPool
176	    except ImportError as e:
177	        raise ImportError(
178	            "langgraph-checkpoint-postgres and psycopg[pool] are required for PostgresSaver. "
179	            "Run: pip install 'langgraph-checkpoint-postgres' 'psycopg[binary,pool]'"
180	        ) from e
181	
182	    conn_str = get_postgres_connection_string()
183	    try:
184	        # A single raw Connection has no recovery: when the Postgres backend goes
185	        # away (node sleep/restart, idle timeout, network blip) the cached
186	        # connection is permanently broken and every subsequent checkpoint op
187	        # raises — silent loss of durability exactly during interrupt/resume across
188	        # the approval gates. Use a self-healing ConnectionPool instead: it runs a
189	        # liveness check on checkout and discards/replaces dead connections.
190	        # PostgresSaver natively accepts a ConnectionPool. (Hardening pass 1, #4.)
191	        pool = ConnectionPool(
192	            conn_str,
193	            min_size=1,
194	            max_size=8,
195	            kwargs={
196	                "autocommit": True,
197	                "prepare_threshold": 0,
198	                "row_factory": dict_row,
199	            },
200	            check=ConnectionPool.check_connection,
201	            open=True,
202	        )
203	        # PostgresSaver does not own the pool, so close it cleanly at process exit
204	        # (otherwise the pool's worker thread cannot be joined during interpreter
205	        # finalization, raising a noisy PythonFinalizationError). A single shared
206	        # atexit handler closes all pools — no per-call accumulation (#249).
207	        _register_pool_cleanup(pool)
208	        # Serialize schema migrations across processes with a session advisory
209	        # lock (pass-1 #33). The lock and the migration DDL MUST run on the SAME
210	        # connection — pg_advisory_lock is session-scoped, and a pool-backed
211	        # PostgresSaver.setup() would check out a *different* connection for the
212	        # DDL, leaving the lock ineffective (pass-3 #2). So run setup() through a
213	        # saver bound to the single locked connection, then return the pool-backed
214	        # saver for normal operation.
215	        # Bootstrap the schema once per process per conn_str. setup() is idempotent
216	        # but a redundant advisory lock + migration SELECT on every run start is
217	        # wasteful; the lock still protects the first-time cross-process race (#276).
218	        # _init_lock makes the in-process check-and-set atomic so concurrent threads
219	        # don't both run setup() (pass-5); the held lock also serializes the DDL,
220	        # which is fine — it runs at most once per conn_str per process.
221	        with _init_lock:
222	            if conn_str not in _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS:
223	                with pool.connection() as conn:
224	                    conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
225	                    try:
226	                        PostgresSaver(conn).setup()  # DDL on the locked connection
227	                    finally:
228	                        # Best-effort unlock: if this raised it would MASK a setup()
229	                        # exception (the one worth surfacing). The advisory lock is
230	                        # session-scoped and auto-releases when the connection returns
231	                        # to the pool, so swallowing here is safe (pass-7).
232	                        try:
233	                            conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
234	                        except Exception:
235	                            pass
236	                _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS.add(conn_str)
237	        return PostgresSaver(pool)
238	    except Exception as e:
239	        # Redact credentials from BOTH the conn_str and the backend exception text
240	        # (psycopg/psycopg_pool can embed the full URI with user:password in their
241	        # own messages), and raise `from None` so the original exception's text is
242	        # not re-exposed through the __cause__ chain (pass-10 + its review).
243	        raise RuntimeError(
244	            f"PostgresSaver connection failed. Is Postgres running on "
245	            f"{_sanitize_conn_str(conn_str)}? Error: {_redact_creds(str(e), conn_str)}\n"
246	            "For Phase 0 testing without Postgres, use get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)."
247	        ) from None
248

1	"""
2	Phoenix observability bootstrap for HC-GRC (roadmap Phase 0, deliverable #2).
3	
4	ADR-0009: Phoenix is the local, self-hosted LLM-tracing stack (LangSmith was
5	disqualified as SaaS-only). The Phoenix server runs on the compute node and
6	agent LLM calls export OpenTelemetry traces to it.
7	
8	Two responsibilities, deliberately separated:
9	
10	  1. The SERVER is a long-lived process — run it via the `phoenix serve` CLI
11	     (see launch_server_command()) or the launchd plist in scripts/. It is NOT
12	     launched from inside the application process.
13	
14	  2. INSTRUMENTATION is wired per-process via bootstrap_observability(), called
15	     once by each run entrypoint. It registers a global OTel tracer provider
16	     (BatchSpanProcessor — spans are buffered and exported in the background, so
17	     a momentary server outage drops spans rather than blocking or failing the
18	     run) pointed at the running server, and turns on the LangChain instrumentor.
19	     Because the provider is registered globally, the spans the reasoning_client
20	     emits for BOTH tiers — including Tier-3 (claude-agent-sdk), which LangChain
21	     instrumentation does not see — export to Phoenix too.
22	
23	run_id propagation (ADR-0015 #79): wrap a run in run_trace_context(run_id) so
24	every span produced during the run carries the run_id as session.id + metadata,
25	cross-referencing MLflow / PostgresSaver / PROV-DM.
26	"""
27	
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import atexit
31	import logging
32	import os
33	import signal
34	import sys
35	import threading
36	from contextlib import contextmanager
37	
38	# ADR-0002: traces carry SCF-derived content and must never leave the machine.
39	# The loopback guard is the single canonical validator in config.py, shared with
40	# Qdrant and MLflow (pass-5) so the data-sovereignty boundary is enforced
41	# identically everywhere; 0.0.0.0 is rejected (it binds all interfaces).
42	from ..config import LOOPBACK_HOSTS as _LOOPBACK_HOSTS  # noqa: F401  (kept for callers/tests)
43	from ..config import load_platform_config
44	from ..config import require_loopback as _require_loopback
45	
46	_log = logging.getLogger(__name__)
47	
48	# Cached registered provider — registration is idempotent within a process
49	# (repeat bootstrap calls return the same provider instead of double-registering
50	# the instrumentor, which would duplicate every span).
51	_PROVIDER = None
52	
53	
54	def _phoenix_cfg() -> dict:
55	    return load_platform_config()["observability"]["phoenix"]
56	
57	
58	def phoenix_base_url() -> str:
59	    """Return the Phoenix server base URL, e.g. http://localhost:6006."""
60	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
61	    return f"http://{cfg.get('host', 'localhost')}:{cfg.get('port', 6006)}"
62	
63	
64	def phoenix_endpoint() -> str:
65	    """Return the OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint the tracer provider exports to.
66	
67	    Validates loopback at the source (pass-5 #obs-wiring): this is public API and
68	    is called from the __main__ entrypoint and tests, not only from the already-
69	    guarded instrument_langchain(), so it must enforce its own ADR-0002 precondition."""
70	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
71	    _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "Phoenix OTLP export")
72	    return f"{phoenix_base_url()}/v1/traces"
73	
74	
75	def launch_server_command() -> str:
76	    """Return the shell command that starts the persistent Phoenix server.
77	
78	    Validates the bind host is loopback (#283): the server bind, not just the
79	    client export host, is what determines whether traces are reachable off-box.
80	    """
81	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
82	    host = _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "server bind")
83	    return f"phoenix serve --host {host} --port {cfg.get('port', 6006)}"
84	
85	
86	def instrument_langchain(project_name: str | None = None):
87	    """
88	    Register a global OTel tracer provider pointed at the Phoenix server (using a
89	    background BatchSpanProcessor) and enable LangChain tracing for this process.
90	
91	    Idempotent: the first call registers; subsequent calls return the cached
92	    provider without re-instrumenting (so spans are never duplicated).
93	
94	    Returns the tracer provider.
95	    """
96	    global _PROVIDER
97	    if _PROVIDER is not None:
98	        return _PROVIDER
99	
100	    from openinference.instrumentation.langchain import LangChainInstrumentor
101	    from opentelemetry import trace as _trace
102	    from phoenix.otel import register
103	
104	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
105	    # ADR-0002 data sovereignty: refuse to export SCF-derived traces off-box.
106	    _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "OTLP export")
107	
108	    # Bound the OTLP/HTTP exporter so a downed Phoenix server cannot block process
109	    # teardown for the exporter's default 10s on the shutdown flush. The installed
110	    # OTel BatchProcessor.force_flush() ignores its timeout_millis argument, so the
111	    # only effective cap is at the exporter level (#277). Don't clobber an explicit
112	    # operator override.
113	    os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2")
114	
115	    provider = register(
116	        project_name=project_name or cfg.get("project_name", "hc-grc"),
117	        endpoint=phoenix_endpoint(),
118	        batch=True,                        # BatchSpanProcessor: buffer + async export
119	        set_global_tracer_provider=True,   # so reasoning_client spans export too
120	    )
121	    # set_tracer_provider can only succeed once per process; if a competing global
122	    # provider already won the race, register() still RETURNS the Phoenix provider
123	    # (so LangChain spans, passed it explicitly, still reach Phoenix) but the GLOBAL
124	    # provider is something else — and reasoning_client's module-level tracer resolves
125	    # through the global, so Tier-3 frontier spans (their ONLY record) would silently
126	    # vanish. Surface that collision instead of dropping them silently (#278).
127	    if _trace.get_tracer_provider() is not provider:
128	        _log.warning(
129	            "Phoenix did not become the global OTel tracer provider — a competing "
130	            "provider was registered first. Tier-3 reasoning_client spans will not "
131	            "reach Phoenix. Ensure bootstrap_observability() runs before any other "
132	            "OTel instrumentation."
133	        )
134	    LangChainInstrumentor().instrument(tracer_provider=provider, skip_dep_check=True)
135	    # Flush buffered spans at process exit so the BatchSpanProcessor does not drop
136	    # the tail of a run on a clean exit / SIGINT (#215). atexit does NOT run on
137	    # SIGTERM (launchd/kill/container-stop), so also install a SIGTERM handler that
138	    # flushes then exits — otherwise the tail of a killed run is silently lost
139	    # (#281). Both flushes are bounded by the exporter timeout set above (#277).
140	    if hasattr(provider, "force_flush"):
141	        atexit.register(provider.force_flush)
142	        _install_sigterm_flush(provider)
143	    _PROVIDER = provider
144	    return provider
145	
146	
147	def _install_sigterm_flush(provider) -> None:
148	    """Flush + shut down the span processor on SIGTERM before the process exits.
149	
150	    Signal handlers can only be installed from the main thread; if we're not on it
151	    (e.g. instrumentation wired from a worker), skip silently — atexit still covers
152	    the clean-exit and SIGINT paths."""
153	    if threading.current_thread() is not threading.main_thread():
154	        return
155	
156	    def _handler(_signum, _frame):
157	        try:
158	            provider.force_flush()
159	            if hasattr(provider, "shutdown"):
160	                provider.shutdown()
161	        finally:
162	            sys.exit(0)
163	
164	    try:
165	        signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handler)
166	    except (ValueError, OSError):  # pragma: no cover - non-main-thread / unsupported
167	        pass
168	
169	
170	def bootstrap_observability():
171	    """
172	    Idempotently wire tracing for the current process. Called once by each run
173	    entrypoint (graph runners). Degrades to a no-op — never raising — when tracing
174	    is disabled by config, switched off via HCGRC_DISABLE_TRACING, or the
175	    instrumentation stack cannot be initialized. Returns the provider or None.
176	    """
177	    if os.environ.get("HCGRC_DISABLE_TRACING", "").strip():
178	        return None
179	    try:
180	        cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
181	    except Exception:
182	        return None
183	    if not cfg.get("enabled", True):
184	        return None
185	    # Also point MLflow at the local store so experiment tracking is actually
186	    # configured in a run path (cross-store run_id correlation, #282). Best-effort.
187	    try:
188	        from ..tracking.mlflow_setup import configure_mlflow
189	        configure_mlflow()
190	    except Exception:
191	        pass
192	    try:
193	        return instrument_langchain()
194	    except Exception:
195	        # Missing deps, server unreachable at registration, etc. — tracing is
196	        # best-effort observability and must never block the research run.
197	        return None
198	
199	
200	@contextmanager
201	def run_trace_context(run_id: str | None):
202	    """
203	    Context manager that stamps run_id onto every span produced within it, for
204	    cross-store correlation. Sets it two ways so BOTH span sources pick it up:
205	    OpenInference using_attributes (LangChain auto-spans → session.id) AND OTel
206	    baggage `hcgrc.run_id`, which the reasoning_client seam reads as a fallback so
207	    its manual T2/T3 spans carry the run_id even when the caller didn't pass it
208	    explicitly (#239). No-op if run_id is None or the helpers are unavailable.
209	    """
210	    if not run_id:
211	        yield
212	        return
213	    from contextlib import ExitStack
214	
215	    from opentelemetry import baggage, context
216	
217	    with ExitStack() as stack:
218	        try:
219	            from openinference.instrumentation import using_attributes
220	            stack.enter_context(using_attributes(session_id=run_id, metadata={"run_id": run_id}))
221	        except Exception:
222	            pass
223	        token = context.attach(baggage.set_baggage("hcgrc.run_id", run_id))
224	        try:
225	            yield
226	        finally:
227	            context.detach(token)
228	
229	
230	if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover - operational entrypoint
231	    print(f"Phoenix base URL:    {phoenix_base_url()}")
232	    print(f"OTLP traces endpoint: {phoenix_endpoint()}")
233	    print(f"Start the server with: {launch_server_command()}")
234

1	"""
2	Qdrant connection helpers for HC-GRC.
3	
4	ADR-0002 data sovereignty: Qdrant runs as a local Docker container (see
5	docker-compose.yml) on the Colima runtime. This module only provides connection
6	parameters, a client factory, and a health check — collection creation and
7	population are the Embedding Agent's responsibility (agents/02-data/embedding-agent).
8	
9	Connection parameters come from configs/platform.yaml -> vector_store.
10	"""
11	
12	from __future__ import annotations
13	
14	from typing import Any
15	
16	from ..config import load_platform_config, require_loopback
17	
18	
19	def _cfg() -> dict[str, Any]:
20	    return load_platform_config()["vector_store"]
21	
22	
23	def qdrant_url() -> str:
24	    """Return the Qdrant REST base URL, e.g. http://localhost:6333."""
25	    cfg = _cfg()
26	    return f"http://{cfg.get('host', 'localhost')}:{cfg.get('http_port', 6333)}"
27	
28	
29	def expected_collections() -> list[str]:
30	    """Return the platform's declared Qdrant collections."""
31	    return list(_cfg().get("collections", []))
32	
33	
34	def get_qdrant_client():
35	    """
36	    Construct a QdrantClient from platform config.
37	
38	    Requires the `qdrant-client` package and a running Qdrant container.
39	    """
40	    cfg = _cfg()
41	    # ADR-0002: embeddings are SCF-derived — refuse to ship them to a non-loopback
42	    # Qdrant, mirroring the Phoenix guard (pass-5). config.py validates at load; this
43	    # is the point-of-use guard so any code path that builds a client is covered.
44	    # Validate BEFORE importing the optional client so the guard fires regardless of
45	    # whether qdrant-client is installed.
46	    host = require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "vector_store")
47	    from qdrant_client import QdrantClient
48	
49	    if cfg.get("prefer_grpc", False):
50	        return QdrantClient(
51	            host=host,
52	            grpc_port=cfg.get("grpc_port", 6334),
53	            prefer_grpc=True,
54	        )
55	    return QdrantClient(host=host, port=cfg.get("http_port", 6333))
56	
57	
58	def qdrant_health() -> dict[str, Any]:
59	    """
60	    Probe the running Qdrant instance.
61	
62	    Returns a dict with reachability, the server-reported collection names, and
63	    which expected collections are still missing. Raises if the client package
64	    is absent or the server is unreachable.
65	    """
66	    client = get_qdrant_client()
67	    present = sorted(c.name for c in client.get_collections().collections)
68	    expected = expected_collections()
69	    return {
70	        "url": qdrant_url(),
71	        "reachable": True,
72	        "collections_present": present,
73	        "collections_missing": [c for c in expected if c not in present],
74	    }
75	
76	
77	if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover - operational entrypoint
78	    import json
79	
80	    print(json.dumps(qdrant_health(), indent=2))
81

1	"""
2	MLflow tracking setup for HC-GRC (roadmap Phase 0, deliverable #1).
3	
4	DATA SOVEREIGNTY (ADR-0002): MLflow runs against a LOCAL SQLITE backend — a
5	single file on the compute node (configs/platform.yaml ->
6	observability.mlflow.tracking_uri, default "sqlite:///mlflow.db"). No remote
7	tracking server, no SaaS — nothing leaves the machine.
8	
9	(MLflow 3 put the legacy filesystem store, e.g. "mlruns/", into maintenance
10	mode; it refuses writes without MLFLOW_ALLOW_FILE_STORE=true and receives no
11	new features. SQLite is the supported local backend and is equally
12	data-sovereign — one file, no daemon.)
13	
14	The SQLite backend needs NO running daemon: experiment data is written
15	directly to the .db file. A server is only needed to *view* runs, via
16	`mlflow ui --backend-store-uri <uri>` (see launch_ui_command()).
17	
18	run_id propagation (ADR-0015 #79): callers should tag every run with the
19	platform run_id so it cross-references PostgresSaver/Phoenix/PROV-DM:
20	    mlflow.set_tag("run_id", state["run_id"])
21	"""
22	
23	from __future__ import annotations
24	
25	import os
26	from pathlib import Path
27	
28	import mlflow
29	
30	from ..config import load_platform_config, repo_root, require_local_uri
31	
32	# Any non-sqlite backing-store URI passes through untouched. Includes DB schemes
33	# (postgres/postgresql/mysql/mssql) — a bare `postgres://` was previously not
34	# recognized and got mangled into a bogus local path (#253).
35	_REMOTE_SCHEMES = (
36	    "http://", "https://", "file:",
37	    "postgresql:", "postgres:", "mysql:", "mysql+", "mssql:", "mssql+",
38	)
39	_SQLITE_PREFIX = "sqlite:///"
40	
41	
42	def get_tracking_uri() -> str:
43	    """
44	    Resolve the MLflow tracking URI from platform config.
45	
46	    A relative SQLite path ("sqlite:///mlflow.db") or a bare relative directory
47	    ("mlruns") is resolved to an absolute location under the repo root, so the
48	    store is independent of the working directory. Absolute paths and remote
49	    schemes (http:, postgresql:, file:) pass through untouched.
50	    """
51	    cfg = load_platform_config()["observability"]["mlflow"]
52	    uri = cfg.get("tracking_uri", f"{_SQLITE_PREFIX}mlflow.db")
53	    if uri.startswith(_SQLITE_PREFIX):
54	        path = uri[len(_SQLITE_PREFIX):]
55	        if not os.path.isabs(path):
56	            path = str(repo_root() / path)
57	        return f"{_SQLITE_PREFIX}{path}"
58	    if uri.startswith(_REMOTE_SCHEMES) or os.path.isabs(uri):
59	        return uri
60	    return str(repo_root() / uri)
61	
62	
63	def configure_mlflow() -> str:
64	    """
65	    Point MLflow at the local store and select the platform experiment.
66	
67	    Idempotent — safe to call at the start of every run. Returns the resolved
68	    tracking URI for logging/verification.
69	    """
70	    cfg = load_platform_config()["observability"]["mlflow"]
71	    uri = get_tracking_uri()
72	    # ADR-0002 defense-in-depth: refuse to point MLflow at a remote tracking server
73	    # even if config validation was bypassed — experiment metadata is SCF-derived
74	    # and must not leave the box (pass-5). config.py validates the raw value at load;
75	    # this guards the resolved value at the actual set_tracking_uri() call site.
76	    require_local_uri(uri, "observability.mlflow")
77	    # A bare local directory (legacy file store) must exist; sqlite/remote do not.
78	    if not uri.startswith(_SQLITE_PREFIX) and not uri.startswith(_REMOTE_SCHEMES):
79	        Path(uri).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
80	    mlflow.set_tracking_uri(uri)
81	    mlflow.set_experiment(cfg.get("experiment_name", "hc-grc"))
82	    return uri
83	
84	
85	def launch_ui_command() -> str:
86	    """Return the shell command to view runs in the MLflow UI."""
87	    return f"mlflow ui --backend-store-uri {get_tracking_uri()}"
88	
89	
90	if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover - operational entrypoint
91	    resolved = configure_mlflow()
92	    print(f"MLflow configured. tracking_uri={resolved}")

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3	# Phoenix observability server — launchd service manager for the compute node.
4	#
5	# Phoenix needs a long-lived server process (unlike MLflow's serverless SQLite
6	# backend). Per ADR-0014 the compute node runs always-on, so Phoenix is managed
7	# as a launchd LaunchAgent: RunAtLoad + KeepAlive, surviving logout and reboot.
8	#
9	# Usage: scripts/infra/phoenix-service.sh {install|uninstall|status|restart|logs}
10	#
11	# The plist is generated into ~/Library/LaunchAgents with absolute paths derived
12	# from this repo's location, so nothing user-specific is committed to the repo.
13	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
14	set -euo pipefail
15	
16	LABEL="com.hcgrc.phoenix"
17	REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)"
18	VENV_PHOENIX="$REPO_ROOT/.venv/bin/phoenix"
19	STORAGE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/phoenix_storage"
20	LOG_FILE="$STORAGE_DIR/phoenix_server.log"
21	PLIST="$HOME/Library/LaunchAgents/$LABEL.plist"
22	
23	# Host/port are read from configs/platform.yaml so the service tracks config.
24	read_cfg() {
25	  "$REPO_ROOT/.venv/bin/python" - "$1" <<'PY'
26	import sys
27	from src.infrastructure.config import load_platform_config
28	load_platform_config.cache_clear()
29	print(load_platform_config()["observability"]["phoenix"][sys.argv[1]])
30	PY
31	}
32	
33	write_plist() {
34	  local host port
35	  host="$(cd "$REPO_ROOT" && read_cfg host)"
36	  port="$(cd "$REPO_ROOT" && read_cfg port)"
37	  # ADR-0002 data sovereignty (#283): the server bind — not just the client export
38	  # host — is what determines whether traces are reachable off-box. Refuse any
39	  # non-loopback bind so a config edit to 0.0.0.0 / a LAN IP cannot expose the
40	  # trace UI/OTLP collector (SCF-derived span content) to the network.
41	  case "$host" in
42	    localhost|127.0.0.1|::1) ;;
43	    *)
44	      echo "ERROR: Phoenix bind host '$host' is not loopback (ADR-0002). Set" \
45	           "observability.phoenix.host to localhost/127.0.0.1/::1 in configs/platform.yaml." >&2
46	      exit 1
47	      ;;
48	  esac
49	  mkdir -p "$STORAGE_DIR" "$(dirname "$PLIST")"
50	  cat > "$PLIST" <<PLISTEOF
51	<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
52	<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
53	<plist version="1.0">
54	<dict>
55	  <key>Label</key><string>$LABEL</string>
56	  <key>ProgramArguments</key>
57	  <array>
58	    <string>$VENV_PHOENIX</string>
59	    <string>serve</string>
60	    <string>--host</string><string>$host</string>
61	    <string>--port</string><string>$port</string>
62	  </array>
63	  <key>EnvironmentVariables</key>
64	  <dict>
65	    <key>PHOENIX_WORKING_DIR</key><string>$STORAGE_DIR</string>
66	  </dict>
67	  <key>WorkingDirectory</key><string>$REPO_ROOT</string>
68	  <key>RunAtLoad</key><true/>
69	  <key>KeepAlive</key><true/>
70	  <key>StandardOutPath</key><string>$LOG_FILE</string>
71	  <key>StandardErrorPath</key><string>$LOG_FILE</string>
72	</dict>
73	</plist>
74	PLISTEOF
75	  echo "Wrote $PLIST (host=$host port=$port)"
76	}
77	
78	case "${1:-}" in
79	  install)
80	    [ -x "$VENV_PHOENIX" ] || { echo "ERROR: $VENV_PHOENIX not found. Run 'make install' first."; exit 1; }
81	    write_plist
82	    launchctl unload "$PLIST" 2>/dev/null || true
83	    launchctl load "$PLIST"
84	    echo "Phoenix service loaded. Tail logs: $0 logs"
85	    ;;
86	  uninstall)
87	    launchctl unload "$PLIST" 2>/dev/null || true
88	    rm -f "$PLIST"
89	    echo "Phoenix service unloaded and plist removed."
90	    ;;
91	  restart)
92	    launchctl kickstart -k "gui/$(id -u)/$LABEL"
93	    echo "Phoenix service restarted."
94	    ;;
95	  status)
96	    launchctl list | grep "$LABEL" || echo "$LABEL not loaded."
97	    ;;
98	  logs)
99	    tail -n 40 -f "$LOG_FILE"
100	    ;;
101	  *)
102	    echo "Usage: $0 {install|uninstall|status|restart|logs}"; exit 1
103	    ;;
104	esac
105

303	def _is_metered_anthropic_credential(key: str) -> bool:
304	    """True for any env var the Anthropic SDK reads as a METERED credential.
305	
306	    The SDK reads several — ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN,
307	    ANTHROPIC_FOUNDRY_API_KEY — and may add more. Rather than chase a static list
308	    (which let ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN, then ANTHROPIC_FOUNDRY_API_KEY, slip through),
309	    match the shape: any ANTHROPIC_* var naming a KEY or TOKEN (pass-11 + review).
310	    CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN — the SUBSCRIPTION auth the CLI must use — does not start
311	    with ANTHROPIC_, so it is deliberately preserved.
312	    """
313	    return key.startswith("ANTHROPIC_") and ("KEY" in key or "TOKEN" in key)
314
